introSection: Sea Viper was a steel roller coaster previously located at Sea World in Gold Coast, Queensland, Australia.

sections.title: History
sections.content: In 1982, Corkscrew opened. For the 2010 season, Corkscrew was renamed Sea Viper. It received new trains from KumbaK.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Orange track and white supports. The ride used to feature white track, but was repainted in 2010.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The ride opened with trains from Arrow. In 2010, these were replaced with trains from KumbaK.

fullText:
Sea Viper was a steel roller coaster previously located at Sea World in Gold Coast, Queensland, Australia.

In 1982, Corkscrew opened. For the 2010 season, Corkscrew was renamed Sea Viper. It received new trains from KumbaK.



Orange track and white supports. The ride used to feature white track, but was repainted in 2010.

The ride opened with trains from Arrow. In 2010, these were replaced with trains from KumbaK.
